changelog.notes: Pass 1 — convergence impossible. 43 clusters from Loop 2 analyzed. MARKETS=0, BREAKING=0, CULTURE=1, AI=3 (all single-source). AI major development (OpenAI Erdős) identified but not yet multi-source confirmed. Gap-fill searches completed for all missing regions. Key missing stories: (1) MARKETS: Dell surge, record S&P/Nasdaq closes, BNB rally, KOSPI foreign selling; (2) SCIENCE: Australian brain-cell Doom research, Typhoon Jangmi (Asia); (3) LATIN AMERICA: Argentina $8M crypto fraud crackdown; (4) EUROPE: Armenia election; (5) AI: Anthropic $965B valuation.
Minimum 3 passes required for convergence. At pass 1, this is a category balance audit only. Convergence evaluation deferred to pass 3+.

WORLD (13) + US_POLITICS (11) = 24/43 clusters (56%). Not yet over the 8-story-per-category threshold individually, but combined dominance of these two categories with ongoing Iran/Israel conflict coverage suggests the briefing may over-index on this news cycle. Flagged for adversarial pass.

Israel-Lebanon Litani River crossing + Beaufort Castle capture is a rapidly escalating, live crisis event. Per AGENT.md: 'BREAKING events always get a ranking boost'. Currently classified WORLD. Loop 7 (final_lock) should assess BREAKING reclassification.

Many clusters (especially AI, SCIENCE, ECONOMY, TECH) remain single-source (independent_count=1). These are flagged for confirmation in Loop 2 dedup on next pass before scoring. Do NOT promote single-source stories to top 30.
